patterns.name: Command Injection (exec)
patterns.description: Potential command injection via exec() with dynamic input
patterns.remediation: Use parameterized commands or allowlisted inputs. Never pass unsanitized user input to exec().

patterns.name: Command Injection (spawn/child_process)
patterns.description: Potential command injection via spawn() or child_process with template literals
patterns.remediation: Pass arguments as array to spawn(), not as interpolated string.

patterns.name: Command Injection (Python os.system/subprocess)
patterns.description: Python command execution with shell=True or f-string in os.system
patterns.remediation: Use subprocess.run() with shell=False and args as list. Avoid os.system().

patterns.name: Command Injection (PowerShell)
patterns.remediation: Avoid Invoke-Expression. Use direct cmdlet calls with validated parameters.

patterns.name: Unsafe eval()
patterns.description: eval() with dynamic input — code injection risk
patterns.remediation: Remove eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, or safe alternatives.

patterns.name: Unsafe Function Constructor
patterns.description: new Function() with dynamic input — equivalent to eval()
patterns.remediation: Avoid new Function() with dynamic content. Use safer alternatives.

patterns.name: innerHTML with Variable
patterns.description: innerHTML set to variable content — XSS risk
patterns.remediation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML. If HTML is needed, sanitize with DOMPurify.

patterns.name: SQL Injection
patterns.description: SQL query with template literal interpolation — injection risk
patterns.remediation: Use parameterized queries ($1, ?, :param). Never interpolate user input into SQL.

patterns.name: Hardcoded Auth URL
patterns.remediation: Remove credentials from URL. Use separate authentication configuration.

patterns.name: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
patterns.description: MD5 or SHA1 used — cryptographically broken for security purposes
patterns.remediation: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 for security purposes. MD5/SHA1 acceptable only for checksums.
